markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Emit recorder-visible build/reuse/refresh/freshness decisions with pack/source hashes. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: ContextPackRecorderBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ContextPackRecorderBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 5ae15777; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

findings.title: pack decisions reuse ContextBundleRecorded event type, distinguished only by payload.kind
findings.evidence: context_pack_recorder.rs:114 emits KernelEventType::ContextBundleRecorded with payload kind=context_pack_decision; a consumer filtering by event type alone cannot separate pack decisions from bundle records without reading payload.kind.
findings.fix: Acceptable (documented design to avoid a new variant); consumers must filter on payload.kind.

adversarial_review_v2.note: Bounded receipts (decision + pack/source hashes + freshness policy, payload_bounded, no full pack content); pack_hash required; red-team leak control honored.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-142 requires a recorder-visible ContextPack lifecycle bridge that emits bounded EventLedger receipts (build/reuse/refresh/freshness allow|deny/require-rebuild) carrying pack/source hashes and freshness policy but never the full pack payload, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The implementation does exactly this and is exercised end-to-end by a real managed-PostgreSQL integration test.
findings: Impl commit 5ae15777 adds src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_retrieval/context_pack_recorder.rs (167 lines, fully implemented, no placeholders); module wired at knowledge_retrieval/mod.rs:38 (pub mod context_pack_recorder).
ContextPackDecision enum (context_pack_recorder.rs:34-58) covers build/reuse/refresh/freshness_allow/freshness_deny/require_rebuild, matching the MT scope verbatim.

record_context_pack_decision (context_pack_recorder.rs:99-122) validates non-empty pack_hash, builds a real NewKernelEvent via the committed builder API (kernel/mod.rs:515-624, chained .aggregate/.source_component/.payload/.correlation_id all exist and return Self), reuses KernelEventType::ContextBundleRecorded (kernel/mod.rs:217) with payload kind=context_pack_decision (no new kernel variant; documented design), and persists via db.append_kernel_event (real PostgresDatabase impl storage/postgres.rs:13432). Returns stored.event_id as evidence handle.

to_bounded_payload (context_pack_recorder.rs:80-95) emits decision+pack_id+target_ref+pack_hash+source_hashes[]+freshness_policy+linkage+payload_bounded:true and omits any full-context key; unit test asserts payload context/pack_payload keys are None (context_pack_recorder.rs:163-164), honoring the red-team leak control.

Sub-rule 2 satisfied by a REAL managed-PG test: knowledge_retrieval_bridges_tests.rs:181-207 (context_pack_decision_emits_bounded_receipt) gates on MemoryFixture::setup() which connects to real PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg() and writes real workspace/source/span rows (knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:43-93), then calls record_context_pack_decision(&fx.pg.db, ...) and asserts a non-empty event id. skip_if_no_pg! only short-circuits when PG is genuinely absent; it is the standard managed-PG gate, not a mock substitute. Both the recorder and the test file are tracked on branch HEAD (git ls-files).

Spec alignment: current spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:8499 defines ContextPackRecord (DerivedContent) {pack_id, target, pack_artifact, source_hashes[], builder, created_at, version}; the impl captures pack_id, target_ref(=target), pack_hash (bounded hash of pack_artifact), source_hashes[], plus decision lifecycle and freshness policy, consistent with the recorder-visibility intent in 2.6.6.7.14.

No forbidden tech (SQLite/Elastic/Docker/external search) in the recorder; no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/deferred-live/Unavailable escapes reachable from the proof path. The single 'stub' grep hit is a historical doc-comment in the test header referring to 'folded-stub bridges', not code.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): the two other commits matching MT-142 (524184b2, 2ac57c36) belong to WP-KERNEL-005 and only touch a stale ModelSession test literal; they are unrelated to this MT and do not affect the verdict.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
